page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with 16 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz without hyperthreading. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 10% tier. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 297.05 GB/sec for read operations and 137.97 GB/sec for write operations. Single-core performance is robust, highlighted by a strong Geekbench single-core score of 1891.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.33 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 204.41 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, with Passmark database operations scoring in the top 25% tier. However, the instance exhibits performance tradeoffs in heavy computational workloads; LLM inference speeds for text generation on a medium 7B model are average at 18.5 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model is weak at 31.22 tokens/sec, and large 70B model text generation is poor at 2.4 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gb.4xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads without the added expense of local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of high memory bandwidth, dedicated ARMv9 physical cores, and strong database performance makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications. Recommended workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM inference or workloads requiring high local storage IOPS.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and strong uncached memory read bandwidth at 297.05 GB/sec. It also delivers strong database operation performance. However, tradeoffs exist in complex AI tasks, as shown by weak to poor LLM inference speeds on larger models. This instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy, non-storage-reliant workloads, making it an excellent fit for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is an ARMv9-based memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 33 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance for databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is a memory-optimized ARMv9 instance with 16 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory performance.
tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized instance delivering high memory bandwidth and dedicated core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier memory performance with uncached read bandwidth of 297.05 GB/sec
Baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
